publications.title: Discriminative Regularized Input Manifold for Multilayer Perceptron
publications.authors: R. Mondal, T. Pal, and P. Dey
publications.venue: Pattern Recognition
publications.abstract: Proposes a discriminative regularization technique that constrains the input manifold of multilayer perceptrons to improve classification accuracy and generalization. The method leverages manifold geometry to shape decision boundaries, achieving robust performance on benchmark datasets.

publications.title: Symmetric Kullback-Leibler Divergence to Regularize Denoising Auto Encoder for Classification
publications.authors: R. Mondal, P. Dey, and T. Pal
publications.venue: Information Sciences
publications.abstract: Introduces a symmetric Kullback-Leibler divergence-based regularization strategy for denoising autoencoders to improve classification performance. The approach enforces consistency between the encoder's latent distribution and a target distribution, reducing overfitting and enhancing feature quality.

publications.title: Graph Clustering via Similarity Aware Embeddings with an MLP-based Learning Strategy
publications.authors: S. K. Panigrahi, D. Maru, S. Gaurav, P. Dey, and P. Bhattacharjee
publications.venue: Proceedings of the 41st ACM/SIGAPP Symposium on Applied Computing
publications.abstract: Proposes a graph clustering approach that learns similarity-aware node embeddings using an MLP-based strategy. By incorporating structural similarity directly into the embedding process, the method achieves improved cluster cohesion and separation compared to conventional graph clustering techniques.

publications.title: AuLiTENet: A New Lightweight and Time-Efficient CNN Architecture for Micro-Expression Classification Leveraging Data Augmentation
publications.authors: A. Jain, D. Bhakta, and P. Dey
publications.venue: Sādhanā
publications.abstract: Presents AuLiTENet, a novel lightweight and time-efficient CNN architecture for micro-expression recognition. Combined with data augmentation strategies, the model achieves competitive accuracy with significantly reduced computational cost, making it suitable for real-time deployment.

publications.title: Two-Tiered Spatio-Temporal Feature Extraction for Micro-Expression Classification
publications.authors: A. Jain, D. Bhakta, and P. Dey
publications.venue: Journal of Visual Communication and Image Representation
publications.abstract: Proposes a two-tiered framework that extracts spatio-temporal features at multiple granularities for micro-expression classification. The hierarchical approach captures both fine-grained local motion patterns and global temporal dynamics, outperforming single-scale methods on standard benchmarks.
